review_focus: today-log group rows and inline editors

findings.title: EventHabitRow bulk delete fires all deletes in a fire-and-forget loop with no error handling
findings.description: The 'Uncheck' button iterates entries and calls `void onDelete(entry.id)` for each one inside a plain onClick handler — no try/catch, no toast on failure, and no confirmation. This is particularly risky because EventHabitRow can hold multiple entries. If any delete fails (network blip, Convex error), the UI silently stays inconsistent. Compare this to EditableEntryRow's 'Sure?/Yes/No' confirmation gate and the `await onDelete` in FoodProcessingView — the uncheck path skips both protections.
findings.fix: Wrap in an async onClick, await each delete in sequence (or Promise.all), catch errors and toast them. Also consider whether bulk-unchecking all entries without a confirmation prompt is appropriate when there is more than one entry in the group.

findings.title: FoodProcessingView delete has no error handling
findings.description: The delete in FoodProcessingView does `await onDelete(entry.id)` inside an async onClick but is not wrapped in try/catch. A rejected promise (Convex mutation error, network failure) will bubble as an unhandled rejection — nothing is shown to the user. The main EditableEntryRow delete path has the same gap: it awaits onDelete at line 259 without a catch block. This means delete failures are silent in all three call sites in the codebase.
findings.fix: Wrap `await onDelete(...)` in try/catch and call `toast.error(...)` in the catch, consistent with how handleSave treats errors in EditableEntryRow.

findings.title: ActivitySubRow draft is never reset when entry prop changes
findings.description: draftDuration is initialised once from the entry prop via useState. If the parent re-renders with an updated entry (e.g. a background Convex sync updates the duration after the component is already mounted), the draft stays at the stale initialised value. The editor will then show the old value on next open and can silently overwrite the updated value if the user saves without noticing. FluidSubRow and ReproductiveSubRow both pass `onStartEditing` to reset draft state on edit-open; ActivitySubRow and HabitSubRow do not.
findings.fix: Add an `onStartEditing` callback that resets `draftDuration` from the current `entry` prop, matching the pattern in FluidSubRow and WeightSubRow.

findings.title: Draft item React key is unstable and causes input focus loss while typing
findings.description: The key for each draft item row is `draft-${draft.name || i}`. Because draft.name is part of the key, typing in the food name input changes the key on each keystroke, which unmounts and remounts the input, resetting focus and cursor position mid-edit. This is a direct usability breakage for the most-used field in the edit form.
findings.fix: Use a stable key that does not depend on mutable draft state. The simplest fix is to use the index alone (`key={i}`) since draft items are only added/removed, not reordered. Alternatively, assign a stable uuid to each DraftItem on initialisation.

findings.title: buildSaveData always sets resolvedBy: 'user' even on unchanged items, erasing LLM resolution provenance
findings.description: When the user saves a food entry without changing any item names or quantities, buildSaveData still reconstructs every item with `resolvedBy: 'user'`. This overwrites the original `resolvedBy` value (e.g. 'llm' or 'auto'), silently converting every item to user-resolved and losing the provenance information used by insights and confidence displays.
findings.fix: Only set `resolvedBy: 'user'` on items where the draft name actually differs from the original parsedName/name. Preserve the original `resolvedBy` for unchanged items by falling back to `originalItems[i]?.resolvedBy`.

findings.title: buildSaveData falls back to the original weight on invalid input instead of blocking save
findings.description: If the user types a non-numeric or zero value into the weight input, buildSaveData silently falls back to `entry.data.weightKg`. The user sees no error and the save succeeds — but with the original data unchanged while appearing to have saved their edit. This is particularly confusing if the user deliberately cleared the field intending to enter a new value.
findings.fix: Either block the save and show a validation error when the parsed value is not a finite positive number, or disable the Save button when the draft is invalid. Do not silently fall back to old data.

findings.title: buildSaveData falls back to the stored periodStartDate when the draft is empty, hiding a user-clear action
findings.description: If the user clears the period start date field, `draftPeriodStartDate || entry.data.periodStartDate` resolves to the original stored value, silently ignoring the user's intent to clear the field. This makes it impossible to remove an incorrect period start date via the inline editor.
findings.fix: Either allow the empty string to be saved as-is (if the domain type permits it), show a validation error, or handle the clear intent explicitly. At minimum, do not silently restore the previous value.

findings.title: The Uncheck button is positioned inside the expand toggle button's hover/click zone, creating a click-target conflict
findings.description: The main `<button>` for toggling the group has `pr-16` to leave room for the absolute-positioned uncheck button. However the Uncheck button sits at `right-9` with `z-10`, placing it geometrically inside the outer button's bounding box. On small screens or fast taps, users intending to expand/collapse the group may accidentally uncheck (delete) all habit entries because the buttons physically overlap.
findings.fix: Move the Uncheck button outside the toggle button's bounding box, or render it as a separate interactive row element rather than an absolutely-positioned child inside the outer button.

findings.title: WeightGroupRow wraps a plain timestamp in a Tooltip with identical content in trigger and tooltip
findings.description: The tooltip trigger displays `truncatePreviewText(format(latest.timestamp, 'HH:mm'))` which is always short (5 chars), and the tooltip body shows the identical value. The tooltip adds zero information. Contrast with FluidGroupRow where the tooltip reveals the full text that is truncated in the trigger — that use is correct. WeightGroupRow's tooltip is a no-op component add that adds DOM nodes and aria overhead for no benefit.
findings.fix:
Remove the Tooltip wrapper from WeightGroupRow's preview and render the timestamp directly as a plain `

` like ActivityGroupRow does.

findings.title: HabitSubRow enters edit mode but renders no editable fields, producing a UI with Save/Cancel and nothing to edit
findings.description: renderEditFields returns null. EditableEntryRow will still render the date/time inputs, a Save button, and a Cancel button in edit mode. This is correct for time-correction, but the habit edit UI has no label or hint explaining what the user is editing, and there is no way to distinguish 'editing this habit's time' from an empty/broken form.
findings.fix: Add a small read-only label in renderEditFields (e.g. the habit name) so the user understands they are editing the timestamp for that specific habit occurrence. This removes ambiguity in a zero-field edit form.

findings.title: ActivityGroupRow is the only group row button with cursor-pointer; all others rely on the browser default
findings.description: All group row `<button type='button'>` elements are interactive and should have `cursor-pointer` for consistency. ActivityGroupRow has it; FluidGroupRow, FoodGroupRow, HabitGroupRows (both variants), ReproductiveGroupRow, and WeightGroupRow do not. This was likely an accidental omission rather than a design choice.
findings.fix: Add `cursor-pointer` to the button className in all five other group row components.

findings.title: Fluid name input maxLength of 10 may silently truncate real beverage names
findings.description: Common fluids like 'Coconut water', 'Electrolyte drink', or 'Chamomile tea' exceed 10 characters. The input will reject keystrokes beyond the limit without any explanation to the user. This is a low-severity usability edge case, but a limit of 10 is unusually tight for a free-text name field.
findings.fix: Raise maxLength to 40 or higher (matching the 60-char limit on food names in FoodSubRow) and add a visible character counter if truncation matters.

findings.title: Habit config lookup uses an OR condition on two different identity fields
findings.description: Both CounterHabitRow and EventHabitRow resolve the config with `h.id === group.groupKey || h.name === firstHabitData?.name`. The name-fallback exists to handle cases where groupKey doesn't match any config id. If both conditions ever both match different configs (id matches one, name matches another), the first match wins unpredictably. The name fallback is a symptom of the groupKey not reliably being the habit id.
findings.fix: Investigate whether groupKey is always the habit id; if so, drop the name fallback. If not, document why name-matching is needed and add a comment so the next developer understands the invariant.
